data.title: Why Is Micron Stock (MU) Rising in Pre-Market Today, 4/6/26?
data.description:
Shares of Micron (MU) are up by over 3% in pre-market hours on Monday after KeyBanc's five-star-rated analyst John Vinh reiterated his Buy rating. He also maintained a $600 price target, implying over 60% upside from current levels. The analyst raised his estimates, pointing to strong demand for processors and memory chips as a key growth driver. Year-to-date, MU stock has gained over 28%.

data.title: JPMorgan Boss Dimon Warns of "Worse than Expected" Interest Rate Hikes From Iran War
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) chief executive Jamie Dimon has warned that U.S. interest rate hikes could be worse than expected as a result of the Iran war.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Higher Interest Rates

In his annual letter to shareholders today, Dimon said that despite the unsettling landscape, that the U.S. economy continues to be resilient, with consumers still earning and spending and businesses still healthy.

However, he warned that because of the war in Iran, "we additionally face the potential for significant ongoing oil and commodity price shocks, along with the reshaping of global supply chains, which may lead to stickier inflation and ultimately higher interest rates than markets currently expect."
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Is Ripple Cheating XRP Investors? The Money Going into its Treasury System Pays Ripple the Company, Not XRP Token Holders
data.description:
The technical progress behind XRP (XRP-USD) is finally winning over the big banks, but a massive gap is opening between the network's success and the investor's wallet. On April 1, 2026, Ripple officially launched its Treasury Management System (TMS) to help giant corporations manage their money. While this move proves the technology is world-class, it also highlights the "cheating" feeling many investors have about the revenue gap built into the system.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

When a corporation pays to use this new software, the money goes to Ripple the company, not the people holding the token.

Why the $13 Trillion Opportunity Skips the Token

The biggest catch for investors is that Ripple is capturing enterprise value through its private business rather than the public token. The company is currently valued at $50 billion, and its new system targets a $13 trillion corporate treasury market. However, for an XRP holder, this massive volume doesn't translate into direct income because the fees for the software and services stay with the private company.

Because the network itself destroys transaction fees to keep things fast, there is no dividend or profit-sharing for those who own XRP. To reach the long-term price target of $12.60, the market cap would need to climb above $720 billion, a level that only Bitcoin has ever held. Without a way to collect a piece of the daily volume, investors are left waiting for the price to grow through hype alone. This structural gap is exactly why some are looking for different ways to earn from the market's activity.

Ripple Helps Connect the Global Market

Even with the revenue gap, Ripple is moving faster than ever to put its technology into the hands of big business. This month, Argentina's YPF used the network to tokenize $800 million in energy assets, and the ledger hit a record 4.49 million transactions in a single day on April 4.

This global system is now being used by more than 7.7 million wallets, showing that the foundation of world finance is shifting toward the XRP Ledger (XRPL). However, despite this record activity, the token price is still struggling near $1.35, down 29% since the start of the year as global tariffs and geopolitical conflict weigh on the market.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Is Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO) Up Today, 4/6/2026?
data.description:
The Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), which tracks the S&P 500 Index (SPX), rose a modest 0.06% in today's pre-market trading, as investors awaited Tuesday's deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. They also reacted to reports of a possible 45-day ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 0.73% at $108.26 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 1.3% to $110.11 per barrel.

Wall Street capped the holiday-shortened trading week on a positive note. The S&P 500 surged nearly 6%, its best weekly gain since late November, snapping a five-week losing streak. The Dow rose about 3%, while the Nasdaq climbed 4.4%, both breaking recent downtrends amid de-escalation hopes.

Overall, VOO has gained 2.65% over the past five days and 30% over the past year.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 4/6/26 – Stock Futures Climb as Traders Eye Tuesday Strait of Hormuz Deadline
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed early Monday as traders awaited Tuesday's deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Investors also reacted to reports of a possible 45-day ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for QQQ bulls

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.71%, 0.42%, and 0.18%, respectively, at 5:32 a.m. EST on April 6.

On Sunday, President Trump warned that the U.S. could strike Iran's power plants and bridges if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday, raising fears of global oil supply disruptions.

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 2.03% at $109.93 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 2.50% to $109.47 per barrel.

Wall Street capped the holiday-shortened trading week on a positive note. The S&P 500 surged nearly 6%, its best weekly gain since late November, snapping a five-week losing streak. The Dow rose about 3%, while the Nasdaq climbed 4.4%, both breaking recent downtrends amid de-escalation hopes.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield rose to near 4.35% as of the last check. Additionally, the Spot Gold price in U.S. dollars hovered around $4,698 per ounce on Monday.

On the earnings calendar, results from Levi Strauss (LEVI), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Constellation Brands (STZ), Applied Digital Corp. (APLD), Blackberry (BB), and Blackrock (BLK) are due this week.

Elsewhere, major European indexes remained closed for Easter Monday.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets ended mixed on April 6 as investors digested updates on the U.S.-Iran conflict.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index dropped 0.70%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 1.00% and the Shenzhen Component declined 1.26%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei rose 0.55% and the Topix fell 0.01%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Options Volatility and Implied Earnings Moves This Week, April 06 – April 10, 2026
data.description:
This week, several major companies will be reporting earnings. Among them are Delta Air Lines (DAL), Applied Digital Corporation (APLD), BlackRock (BLK), BlackBerry (BB), Constellation Brands (STZ).

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

New trading tool for APLD bulls/bears

Ahead of this week's major earnings announcements, TipRanks has calculated the expected moves of these companies' stocks. We use the at-the-money straddle of the options whose post-earnings expiration dates are closest to the earnings announcement date.

Note that options volatility tends to be high in the days leading up to an earnings announcement, due to uncertainty around the earnings. Investors don't know whether the earnings will be positive or negative, so the price of options (aka the option premium) rises.

Furthermore, after the earnings announcement, because the uncertainty has been resolved, the price of options (the option premium) decreases substantially. This is known as the volatility crush.

data.title: Which Energy ETFs Are Leading 2026 Gains as Oil Prices Surge?
data.description:
Energy ETFs have emerged as the top performers in 2026, with funds like the Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET) and major oil-linked ETFs leading the gains. The rally has been driven by a sharp rise in oil prices.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

While tech stocks face pressure from valuation concerns, investor focus has shifted toward energy and commodity-linked assets. This move is largely tied to supply disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz, a key route for global oil shipments, which has pushed prices higher and lifted returns across the sector.

Tanker ETF Leads with Standout Gains

The biggest gainer this year is the Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET), which is up an exceptional 585% year-to-date. The fund tracks tanker freight rates, which have surged as supply routes face disruption.

Unlike standard oil funds, BWET tracks the cost of transporting crude oil. Because of the conflict with Iran, tankers are either refusing to enter the Strait or charging massive premiums to take the risk. This has caused "freight futures" to explode, handing BWET investors historic gains.

Oil ETFs Near 100% Gains

Crude oil prices have also moved higher as supply tightens, reflecting the impact of ongoing disruptions. This has supported strong gains in oil-linked ETFs, with several popular funds now approaching the 100% mark for the year. As a result, energy-focused investments continue to lead the market.

  • United States Oil Fund (USO): Up 99.42%
  • United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO): Up 91.10%
  • Invesco DB Energy Fund (DBE): Up 73.95%

Unlike previous energy rallies, the current surge is also being driven by a shortage of refined products such as gasoline and diesel. This has pushed funds like the United States Gasoline Fund (UGA) up over 71% this year.

A key factor behind this move is the Strait of Hormuz, through which roughly 20% of the world's oil supply passes. With ongoing disruptions, markets are factoring in potential supply risks. As a result, investors are shifting away from growth stocks toward energy-related assets that tend to benefit in such conditions.

What to Watch Next

The "wait-and-see" phase continues as the Tuesday deadline for reopening the Strait approaches. If a 45-day ceasefire is finalized, these energy gains could cool off rapidly. However, as long as the blockade remains in place, energy and tanker stocks are likely to remain among the few areas showing gains in an otherwise weak market.

data.author: Shalu Saraf
